title: PureBite
summary: A fully responsive e-commerce grocery store web application featuring a modern cart state, secure Razorpay checkout, and responsive design, built with the MERN stack (MongoDB, Express, React, Node.js) with raw JavaScript.
description: A premium, high-converting digital storefront designed for selling organic and vegetarian products. Built with raw JavaScript, persistent cart, and seamless Razorpay gateway.

caseStudy.overview: PureBite is a fast and responsive online grocery shopping storefront specifically optimized for organic and vegetarian products. The platform is engineered to allow users to easily browse fresh products, search for grocery items, filter by category, manage a persistent shopping cart state, and complete payments securely via Razorpay.
caseStudy.challenge: Building a high-performance e-commerce storefront requires rapid page interactions, instant shopping cart additions, and secure state handling on the client side. A major challenge was creating a frictionless, reliable checkout flow integrated with external payment APIs (Razorpay) while maintaining smooth performance across mobile devices.
caseStudy.solution: We designed and implemented a MERN stack (MongoDB, Express, React, Node.js) architecture. The React frontend relies on hooks and state synchronization for immediate cart feedback. Payments are processed through the embedded Razorpay checkout script, communicating securely with our Node.js/Express backend API for payment confirmation and receipt logging. No actual user data is stored insecurely or exposed on the frontend.
results: Instant e-commerce cart calculation and update response times.
Secure payments processed through Razorpay with backend verification.
Client-side rendering enables fast transitions without full page loads.

title: FreshCart Mobile
summary: Cross-platform iOS and Android shopping apps built with Flutter, sharing the same unified Node.js/Postgres API backend to handle real-time inventory and order processing with single-codebase efficiency.
description: Developing cross-platform iOS and Android mobile shopping apps sharing the exact same backend database API as the web platform. Focuses on cart states and order flows.

caseStudy.overview: To complement their web storefront, FreshCart needed native mobile applications for iOS and Android. The apps had to synchronize real-time inventory, user shopping carts, and order states with the same database backend used by the web application.
caseStudy.challenge: Maintaining separate Swift and Kotlin codebases would have doubled development costs and slowed release cycles. We needed a single cross-platform codebase that didn't compromise on native feel, interface smoothness, or complex state management (like local caching of shopping carts and push notification triggers).
caseStudy.solution: We developed native-quality mobile apps using Flutter and Dart. We implemented clean state management using the BLoC pattern, separating UI components from business logic. We unified the authentication layer across web and mobile using secure JWT storage and built custom HTTP middleware to handle offline-first cart syncing.
results: Single codebase reduced overall development time and cost by 50%.
Consistent 60 FPS scrolling and transition performance on both iOS and Android.
Secure local offline storage allows users to browse products and modify carts without active internet.
